seo.title: Texas CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Texas - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity texas, how to transfer CNA license to texas, texas CNA endorsement application

board.name: Texas HHSC
board.mailing_address:
Texas Health and Human Services Commission
Nurse Aide Registry

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Texas.

steps.title: Complete a Texas-Approved Nurse Aide Training Program (NATCEP)
steps.description: Enroll in and successfully complete a Nurse Aide Training and Competency Evaluation Program (NATCEP) approved by the Texas Health and Human Services (HHS).

steps.title: Create an Account and Register for the Competency Exam with Prometric
steps.description: Visit the Prometric Texas Nurse Aide Portal and follow the instructions to create a candidate account and schedule your Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Exam (NACEP).

steps.title: Pass the Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Exam
steps.description: Successfully complete both the written/oral and skills portions of the NACEP administered by Prometric. This exam tests your knowledge and practical skills as a nurse aide.

steps.title: Be Listed on the Texas Nurse Aide Registry
steps.description: Upon passing the NACEP, Prometric will report your results to the Texas Health and Human Services (HHS), and you will be automatically added to the Texas Nurse Aide Registry within 7-10 business days. No separate application is required for initial listing.

steps.title: Maintain Active Registry Status
steps.description: To remain active on the registry, you must work for pay performing nursing-related duties under the supervision of a licensed nurse for at least 16 hours within a 24-month period, and renew your certification every two years.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Texas CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in Texas. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Texas.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Texas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Texas
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) in Texas, candidates must complete an approved training program and pass the state competency exam administered through the Prometric Texas Nurse Aide Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Texas | Full Requirements & Steps
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in Texas. Learn about NATCEP training hours, DPS background check, and the state competency exam.
target_queries: how to become a cna in texas

new_applicant.faq_heading: Common Questions about Getting a CNA in Texas

sections.label: Basic Requirements
sections.body: To enroll in a Texas NATCEP program, you must be 18 years old and pass a DPS criminal background check.

faq.q: How many hours of training do I need to be a CNA in Texas?
faq.a: Texas requires 100 hours of training: 60 hours of classroom instruction and 40 hours of clinical practice in a state-approved NATCEP.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Prometric Texas Nurse Aide website, navigate to 'Schedule your exam' or 'Online Application', and follow the prompts to create a candidate account and register for the competency evaluation.
